response: Val cuts a single watermelon into 40 slices, and each slice has 20 black seeds and 20 white seeds, which means every piece contains a total of 40 seeds, a fact that might make your tongue tingle with curiosity. Since there are 40 slices in the whole fruit, multiplying the 40 seeds per slice by the 40 slices gives us a grand total of 1600 seeds in the entire watermelon, a number that could make your tongue dance with delight.
